title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.1 Define ToolDefinition generic interface
notes: ToolDefinition<T> generic interface already fully implemented in Section 1 of open-spec-agent.ts (lines 48-57). Verified implementation satisfies SPEC-REGISTRY-001: name, description, schema (ZodTypeAny), and execute fields present.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-4.6 Apply consistent emoji prefix format to all output
notes: Fixed emoji prefix format in runAgent: (1) changed 'Calling tool:' to '🔧 Tool:' on tool dispatch, (2) added '📋 Result:' log after successful tool execution, (3) added '🔍' prefix to both DEBUG request and DEBUG response headers. All 4 tests pass.

title: [wrapper][P2] T-2.5 Smoke-test ai-powered client wiring
notes: Added T-2.5 test in open-spec-agent.test.ts. Imports getAiClient from ai-powered, creates a mock-mode client via await getAiClient('T-2.5-smoke', { mock: true }), calls generateText('Say hello.'), and asserts result.content is a non-empty string. Test passes without any API key.

title: [wrapper][P1] T-1.1 Add ESM module type to package.json
notes: Created package.json with "type": "module" to enable ESM for Node 20+ LTS.

title: [wrapper][P1] T-1.2 Install runtime dependencies
notes: Ran npm install ai-powered zod. ai-powered@0.1.0 and zod@3.25.76 added to dependencies.

title: [wrapper][P1] T-1.3 Install dev dependencies
notes: Ran npm install --save-dev tsx typescript @types/node. tsx@4.21.0, typescript@6.0.2, @types/node@25.5.0 added to devDependencies.

title: [wrapper][P1] T-1.6 Add .env files to .gitignore
notes: Replaced empty .gitignore with entries for node_modules/, dist/, .env, and .env* so API keys and build artefacts are never committed. Ref: design.md Security Design.

title: [wrapper][P2] T-2.3 Add API key startup validation
notes: Created open-spec-agent.ts with AgentConfig interface and resolveConfig(). Validation block in resolveConfig() exits with code 1 and prints a clear error when provider != 'ollama' and apiKey is empty. Satisfies SPEC-AGENT-008, UT-CONFIG-003, IT-008.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.3 Define ExecuteOpenspecSchema with Zod
notes: Defined ExecuteOpenspecSchema in open-spec-agent.ts using z.object with command as z.enum(['propose','apply','archive','status','list','init']), optional args as z.array(z.string()), and optional flags as z.record(z.string(),z.string()). Satisfies SPEC-TOOL-001, UT-CMD-001 through UT-CMD-004.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-4.1 Define system prompt constant
notes: Added SYSTEM_PROMPT exported const to Section 4 of open-spec-agent.ts. Matches the exact skeleton mandated by wrapper-JIRA.md §2c and design.md System Prompt Design. Covers: agent identity, 4-stage propose/review/apply/archive workflow, and 5 hard rules (no step skipping, no fabrication, surface exact stderr, ask for clarification on ambiguous input, end with status summary). Well within the ≤400 token budget. Satisfies SPEC-AGENT-001, AC-2, AC-3, AC-9.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-4.5 Implement main entry point
notes: Implemented async main() in Section 5 of open-spec-agent.ts. Reads process.argv[2]; prints usage hint and exits 1 when missing. Calls resolveConfig() then runAgent(). Top-level .catch() handler prints error to stderr and exits 1 for all unhandled rejections. Satisfies SPEC-AGENT-007, IT-008, AC-1.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.8 Implement dispatchTool function
notes: Implemented dispatchTool(name, rawInput) in Section 3 of open-spec-agent.ts. Registry lookup throws on unknown name with list of available tools. Zod safeParse validates input and throws on failure. Calls tool.execute() and returns the string result. Satisfies SPEC-DISPATCH-001, UT-DISPATCH-001 through UT-DISPATCH-003.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.5 Implement executeOpenspec function
notes: Implemented shellQuote, scrubStderr, and executeOpenspec in Section 3 of open-spec-agent.ts. Also registered execute_openspec in the global registry (bd-re1e). shellQuote wraps values in double-quotes with backslash escaping. scrubStderr redacts sk-* and Bearer * patterns per-line. executeOpenspec builds the CLI command from whitelisted parts, logs with '🔧 Tool:' prefix, runs with 60s timeout via child_process.exec, returns JSON-serialised ToolResult. Satisfies SPEC-TOOL-002, SPEC-TOOL-004 through SPEC-TOOL-008, SPEC-REGISTRY-001, SPEC-REGISTRY-002.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-4.3 Implement runAgent core ReAct loop
notes: Implemented zodToJsonSchema, buildToolSchema (bd-43cy pre-implementation), and runAgent in Section 4 of open-spec-agent.ts. zodToJsonSchema converts ZodString/ZodEnum/ZodArray/ZodRecord/ZodObject/ZodOptional/ZodDefault to Anthropic-compatible JSON Schema. runAgent initialises the Anthropic SDK client from AgentConfig, builds tool schemas from the registry, and runs the ReAct while-loop bounded by maxIterations. Handles tool_use stop_reason by dispatching all tool calls in parallel and appending tool_result blocks in a single user turn. Handles end_turn by printing the final text answer. Emits DEBUG payloads to stderr when config.debug is true (bd-ypnq). Verified zero tsc --noEmit --strict --skipLibCheck errors. Satisfies SPEC-AGENT-002 through SPEC-AGENT-005, IT-001, AC-5, AC-6.

title: [wrapper][P2] T-2.1 Define AgentConfig TypeScript interface
notes: AgentConfig interface was already implemented in open-spec-agent.ts with all 8 required fields: provider, model, apiKey, baseUrl (string|undefined), temperature (number), maxIterations (number), cwd (string), debug (boolean). Exported AnthropicClientLike interface added as a companion type for DI testability. Created tsconfig.json with strict:true, module:Node16, skipLibCheck:true, allowImportingTsExtensions:true, noEmit:true. Added typecheck script to package.json. tsc --noEmit --strict exits 0. Satisfies design.md Config Interface, SPEC-AGENT-008.

title: [wrapper][P5] T-5.1 Run tsc --noEmit --strict and fix all type errors
notes: Resolved all TypeScript errors: added skipLibCheck:true to work around @anthropic-ai/sdk internal type issues, added allowImportingTsExtensions:true and noEmit:true to support .ts extension imports in the test file, tightened runAgent signature to accept AnthropicClientLike for DI. Final tsc --noEmit --strict exits 0 with zero errors. Satisfies AC-7.

title: [wrapper][P5] T-5.2 End-to-end test: verify propose precedes apply
notes: Wrote open-spec-agent.test.ts using node:test and node:assert/strict. Strategy: dependency injection via runAgent's clientOverride parameter (AnthropicClientLike) for the mock Anthropic client; registry.set() override for execute_openspec to capture executed commands without invoking child_process. Mock LLM scripted to emit tool_use(propose) → tool_use(apply) → end_turn. Assertions: LLM called ≥3 times, executedCommands=['propose','apply'] in order, execute_openspec appears in console log, final answer emitted. Runs with: node --experimental-strip-types --test open-spec-agent.test.ts. Result: ✔ T-5.2 propose precedes apply in console output (18ms), 1 pass 0 fail exit 0. Satisfies AC-1, AC-2, IT-001.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.2 Instantiate tool registry Map
notes: registry declared as export const registry = new Map<string, ToolDefinition>() at module scope in open-spec-agent.ts (line 160). All tools are registered into this map before runAgent is called. Implementation was pre-delivered in batch 4 as part of bd-ahr1. Satisfies SPEC-REGISTRY-001, SPEC-REGISTRY-002.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.7 Register execute_openspec in registry
notes: registry.set('execute_openspec', { name, description, schema: ExecuteOpenspecSchema, execute: (input) => executeOpenspec(input, process.env['OPENSPEC_CWD'] ?? process.cwd()) }) called at module scope in open-spec-agent.ts (lines 276-286). Description clearly mandates execute_openspec as the ONLY CLI execution mechanism and lists all valid subcommands. Implementation was pre-delivered in batch 4 as part of bd-ahr1. Satisfies SPEC-REGISTRY-001, SPEC-REGISTRY-002.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-4.2 Implement buildToolSchema function
notes: buildToolSchema(tool: ToolDefinition): Anthropic.Tool exported from open-spec-agent.ts (lines 448-454). Internally uses zodToJsonSchema() (lines 374-438) which converts ZodString, ZodEnum, ZodArray, ZodRecord, ZodObject, ZodOptional, ZodDefault to Anthropic-compatible JSON Schema objects. Returns { name, description, input_schema } matching the Anthropic SDK Tool type. Implementation was pre-delivered in batch 4 as part of bd-1uu0. Satisfies SPEC-REGISTRY-002, SPEC-AGENT-003.

title: [wrapper][P1] T-1.5 Create tsconfig.json
notes: Corrected tsconfig.json to match task spec exactly: strict:true, module:Node16, moduleResolution:Node16, target:ES2022, outDir:dist. Removed allowImportingTsExtensions:true and noEmit:true (added by bd-qqrh) — those options force noEmit globally and break the openspec:agent:build script. Since the test suite runs via 'node --experimental-strip-types' (not tsc), the .ts extension import in the test file works at runtime without allowImportingTsExtensions. Added '*.test.ts' to the tsc exclude list so tsc never sees the .ts-extension import. Verified: npm run typecheck exits 0; npm test exits 0 (1 pass). Satisfies SPEC-AGENT-007, AC-7.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.4 Implement shellQuote helper function
notes: shellQuote(value: string): string is implemented and exported in open-spec-agent.ts (lines 177-179). It was pre-delivered in batch 4 as a dependency of executeOpenspec (bd-ahr1). The implementation wraps the value in double-quotes and escapes embedded backslashes (via /\\/g) then embedded double-quotes (via /"/g), satisfying UT-QUOTE-001 (plain string wrapped), UT-QUOTE-002 (embedded quotes escaped), and UT-QUOTE-003 (empty string returns empty double-quotes). Used in executeOpenspec for all positional args and all flag values to prevent shell injection. Satisfies SPEC-TOOL-003.

title: [wrapper][P5] T-5.5 Update root README with AI Development Tools section
notes: Created README.md in the repository root (file did not previously exist). Contains: project overview, AI Development Tools section with prerequisites table (Node.js, OpenSpec CLI, LLM API key), .env setup block with all 8 env vars and defaults, four invocation examples (full propose workflow, list proposals, archive change, debug mode), NPM scripts table, and a link to openspec/changes/wrapper/README.md for full documentation. Also includes Project Structure section. Satisfies AC-10.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.6 Implement stderr scrubbing
notes: scrubStderr(raw: string): string is implemented and exported in open-spec-agent.ts (lines 198-211). It splits input by newline and checks each line against two patterns: /sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}/ (OpenAI/Anthropic-style secret keys) and /Bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._~+/=-]{8,}/ (Authorization header bearer tokens). Matching lines are replaced with '[REDACTED — potential credential]'; non-matching lines pass through unchanged. Satisfies UT-SCRUB-001 (sk- key redacted), UT-SCRUB-002 (Bearer token redacted), UT-SCRUB-003 (clean lines preserved). Used in executeOpenspec to scrub tool stderr before returning to the LLM. Satisfies SPEC-TOOL-008.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-5.4 Debug mode test
notes: Added test T-5.4 to open-spec-agent.test.ts. The test sets config.debug=true and patches both console.log and console.error simultaneously to capture stdout and stderr independently. Asserts: (1) stderrLines contains a '── DEBUG request' header, (2) stderrLines contains a '── DEBUG response' header, (3) at least one stderrLine starts with '{' (JSON payload serialised), (4) stdoutLines contains the 🚀 agent banner, (5) stdoutLines contains the final agent answer. Both T-5.2 and T-5.4 pass (2 pass, 0 fail). Satisfies AC-6, IT-007.

title: [wrapper][P2] T-1.4 Add npm scripts
notes: Verified that package.json already contains all three required npm scripts: 'openspec:agent' (tsx open-spec-agent.ts), 'openspec:agent:build' (tsc --outDir dist --declaration), and 'openspec:agent:run' (node dist/open-spec-agent.js). These were pre-delivered in earlier batches. No changes required. Satisfies T-1.4 per tasks.md.

title: [wrapper][P2] T-2.2 Implement resolveConfig function
notes: resolveConfig() was already fully implemented in Section 2 of open-spec-agent.ts (lines 96-122) in prior batches. It reads all 8 env vars (AI_PROVIDER default 'openai', AI_MODEL default '', AI_API_KEY default '', AI_BASE_URL optional, AI_TEMPERATURE parsed float default 0.2, AI_MAX_ITERATIONS parsed int default 10, OPENSPEC_CWD default process.cwd(), DEBUG boolean). Includes startup API key validation (bd-fst3). Returns a fully-typed AgentConfig. No new code required. Satisfies UT-CONFIG-001, UT-CONFIG-002.

title: [wrapper][P2] T-2.4 Add openspec CLI availability check
notes: Added exported async checkOpenspecCLI() function to Section 2 of open-spec-agent.ts. Runs 'where openspec' on Windows (process.platform==='win32') or 'which openspec' on all other platforms via child_process.exec. On non-zero exit code prints '❌ openspec CLI not found on PATH.' with 'npm install -g openspec' install hint and calls process.exit(1). On success resolves the returned Promise<void>. Wired into main() immediately after resolveConfig() and before runAgent() so the agent fails fast before any LLM call. Section 2 header updated to list [bd-fst3, bd-1xxf]. Satisfies design.md Startup Sequence, README.md Troubleshooting, T-2.4.

title: [wrapper][P5] T-5.3 Failure test: invalid or missing API key
notes: Added T-5.3 test to open-spec-agent.test.ts. Added MockExitError sentinel class (plain-class syntax — TypeScript parameter properties are unsupported by node --experimental-strip-types). Added resolveConfig to the import. Test: temporarily deletes AI_API_KEY from env, sets AI_PROVIDER to 'openai', mocks process.exit to throw MockExitError (stopping execution at the call site, mirroring real process.exit behaviour), captures console.error lines, calls resolveConfig(), catches MockExitError, then asserts: (1) process.exit called with code 1, (2) stderr mentions 'AI_API_KEY', (3) stderr names provider 'openai'. No LLM call is structurally impossible — resolveConfig() throws before returning, so runAgent() and the Anthropic SDK are never invoked. npm test: 3 pass 0 fail (T-5.2, T-5.3, T-5.4). npm run typecheck: 0 errors. Satisfies AC-3, AC-4, IT-008.

title: [wrapper][P4] T-4.4 Add DEBUG logging to ReAct loop
notes: DEBUG logging was already fully implemented in runAgent (open-spec-agent.ts lines 588-599): when config.debug is true, the full LLM request payload is printed to stderr with '🔍 DEBUG request' header before each LLM call, and the full response payload is printed with '🔍 DEBUG response' header after. Test T-5.4 (bd-evku) was also present and passing, asserting both headers appear in stderr and at least one JSON-object payload is emitted. npm test: 4 pass 0 fail. Satisfies SPEC-AGENT-006, IT-007.

title: [wrapper][P3] T-3.9 Manual integration test for executeOpenspec list
notes: Temporarily added executeOpenspec({ command: 'list' }, config.cwd) call in main() before runAgent. Ran with AI_API_KEY=test-skip. Output confirmed: ToolResult JSON { success: true, command: 'openspec list', stdout: 'Changes:\n  wrapper     0/32 tasks    11h ago', stderr: '' }. stdout correctly captured, success:true, no stderr. Temp call removed before commit. npm test: 4 pass 0 fail. Satisfies IT-002.
